ext_3243: A guess that it will almost certainly be so.
ext_3244: It means that it is correct eight or nine times out of ten, indicating that it will almost certainly be so.
ext_3245: The appearance of four or five gathered together.
ext_3246: A term describing the appearance of four or five moving in a group.
ext_3247: Getting back up after multiple failures.
ext_3248: It means getting up eight times after falling seven times, symbolizing a strong will to rise again despite repeated failures.
ext_3249: Teaching and learning together to grow.
ext_3250: It means that the process of teaching and learning helps both to grow together, originating from the concept of mutual growth.
ext_3251: Maintaining the initial intention until the end.
ext_3252: It means to consistently pursue the intention set at the beginning until the end.
ext_3253: All things and phenomena in the world.
ext_3254: It refers to all things and phenomena densely lined up in the universe, encompassing everything in the world.
ext_3255: A very large number of soldiers and horses. A very strong support.
ext_3256: It means a thousand soldiers and ten thousand horses, metaphorically representing a vast military force or very strong support.
ext_3257: Waiting so long that a day feels like three years.
ext_3258: It means a day feels as long as three autumns, expressing a heart that waits anxiously.
ext_3259: Countless mountains and rivers. A difficult journey.
ext_3260: It means thousands of mountains and tens of thousands of rivers, metaphorically representing a very long and difficult journey.
ext_3261: Everyone has different personalities and opinions.
ext_3262: It means ten people have ten different colors, expressing that everyone has different personalities and thoughts.
ext_3263: A clear and bright midday. Or pure innocence.
ext_3264: It means a blue sky and bright sun, signifying a clear midday or pure innocence without any concealment.
ext_3265: Gaining a large sum of money at once.
ext_3266: It means to grasp a fortune in one action, indicating the desire to obtain great wealth effortlessly in one go.
ext_3267: All are similar, so there is no change.
ext_3268: It means that all writings follow the same rule, indicating that many are similar and lack change or individuality.
ext_3269: A situation isolated with enemies all around.
ext_3270: Referring to the story of Xiang Yu in the Battle of Gaixia, where he became isolated surrounded by enemies, indicating a situation surrounded by foes.
ext_3272: It means sleeping in the same bed but dreaming different dreams, metaphorically indicating that while acting together outwardly, each harbors different thoughts internally.
ext_3274: It means that blue comes from indigo but is bluer than indigo, metaphorically indicating that the disciple has become superior to the master.
ext_3275: Good things are added to good things.
ext_3276: It means adding flowers on silk, indicating that something good is enhanced by something even better.
ext_3277: Not distinguishing between old and young.
ext_3278: It means not distinguishing based on age.
ext_3280: It means living long without aging, embodying the desire for eternal life that humans have dreamed of since ancient times.
ext_3281: Gathering talented individuals and nurturing them correctly.
ext_3282: It means to teach and cultivate outstanding talents.
ext_3283: Gaining two benefits from one action.
ext_3284: It means gaining two advantages from a single action.
ext_3285: A crisis is a matter of a single hair. A very urgent moment.
ext_3286: It means a crisis of a hair's breadth, referring to a precarious moment where one is in great danger by a narrow margin.
ext_3287: Mencius' mother moved three times for her child's education.
ext_3288: This story emphasizes how important the environment is for children's education, as Mencius' mother moved three times in order: near a grave, a market, and a school.
ext_3289: The saying that men from the south and women from the north are beautiful.
ext_3290: This saying comes from the belief that in our country, there are many handsome men in the south and beautiful women in the north.
ext_3291: Widely learned and well-informed. Having broad knowledge and many insights.
ext_3292: It means having wide learning and much knowledge, signifying a rich understanding and broad scholarship.
ext_3293: Everyone has different thoughts, personalities, and tastes.
ext_3294: It means that if there are a hundred people, there are a hundred colors, expressing that everyone has different personalities and thoughts.
ext_3295: Giving an absurd answer when asked about the east.
ext_3296: It means answering west when asked about the east, indicating a completely irrelevant and absurd response.
ext_3297: Conveying truth without words or writing.
ext_3298: In Buddhist Zen teachings, it is said that truth cannot be conveyed through letters or language but is transmitted directly from mind to mind.
ext_3299: Pouring all heart and strength into one task.
ext_3300: It means to devote all heart and strength.
ext_3301: Asking oneself and finding the answer.
ext_3302: It means to ask oneself and answer oneself, describing the act of thinking alone and reaching a conclusion.
ext_3303: Famous and grand mountains and rivers. Natural scenery.
ext_3304: It means renowned mountains and great rivers, referring to outstanding and famous natural landmarks.
ext_3305: The name and actual content match well.
ext_3306: It means that the name and reality correspond exactly, indicating that rumors or reputations align with the actual content.
ext_3308: It means that excess is akin to deficiency, teaching that moderation is important in all matters.
ext_3309: Having many worries both inside and out.
ext_3310: It means having worries inside and external threats, describing a difficult situation both internally and externally.
ext_3311: A bustling procession with carts and horses never ceasing.
ext_3312: It means that carts flow like water and horses follow like dragons, metaphorically describing a very busy and lively scene.
ext_3313: The subject and object are reversed. Important and secondary things are inverted.
ext_3314: It means that the master and guest are reversed, indicating that the order of importance or sequence of things is inverted.
ext_3315: When the husband leads, the wife follows. A harmonious couple.
ext_3316: It means that when the husband sings, the wife follows, signifying a harmonious relationship where the couple adjusts well to each other.
ext_3317: Soft and gentle on the outside, but strong and resilient on the inside.
ext_3318: It means being soft and flexible on the outside but firm and strong on the inside.
ext_3319: Sharing both pain and joy, experiencing life's ups and downs together.
ext_3320: It means sharing both hardships and joys, indicating a deep relationship where difficult and good times are shared together.
ext_3321: A firm resolution that does not last beyond three days and fades away.
ext_3322: It means that a determination does not last beyond three days, indicating that resolutions or willpower do not endure and quickly fade away.
ext_3323: If there is one advantage, there is also one disadvantage.
ext_3324: It means that if there is one advantage, there is also one disadvantage, indicating that everything has both good and bad points.
ext_3325: Considering oneself the best and boasting arrogantly.
ext_3326: It means to consider oneself high and great, referring to an attitude of arrogance.
ext_3327: Means that the means or attitude is correct, honorable, and just.
ext_3328: It means being upright and dignified, indicating that the attitude or means is appropriate and honorable.
ext_3329: Fight a hundred times and win a hundred times. Always victorious.
ext_3330: It means to fight a hundred times and win a hundred times, indicating always winning in war or competition.
ext_3331: Filled with soft and bright energy, harmonious.
ext_3332: It means being filled with harmonious energy, expressing a friendly and joyful atmosphere.
ext_3333: Then and now, in the East or the West, the whole world.
ext_3334: Then and now, it refers to universal truths or principles that apply everywhere.
ext_3336: It means fair, bright, and upright, referring to an attitude that is impartial and honorable.
ext_3337: Many people unite as one heart and one mind.
ext_3338: It means one heart and one body, indicating that many have completely united as one.
ext_3339: Each finds a way to live on their own.
ext_3340: It means that each person seeks their own way to survive, finding their own methods in difficult situations.
ext_3341: Possessing both outstanding talent and beautiful appearance.
ext_3342: It means having both talent and beauty, referring to a person who is both capable and attractive.
ext_3343: Having many talents and outstanding abilities.
ext_3344: It means having many talents and abilities, referring to a person with exceptional skills in various fields.
ext_3345: Shoot a hundred times and hit a hundred times. All predictions come true.
ext_3346: It means shooting a hundred times and hitting a hundred times, indicating that plans or predictions always come true.
ext_3347: Experiencing all kinds of hardships and difficulties.
ext_3348: It means having fought in both mountains and waters, indicating that one has experienced all kinds of hardships and understands the world well.
ext_3349: Building wealth solely through one's own efforts without inheritance.
ext_3350: It means accumulating wealth and achieving success solely through one's own efforts without inheritance.
ext_3351: Many visitors make the front of the house very crowded.
ext_3352: It means that the front door is like a market, indicating that there are so many visitors that it becomes very crowded.
ext_3353: A large and important plan made in advance for the distant future.
ext_3354: It means a grand plan that looks a hundred years ahead, referring to a significant plan made for the distant future.
ext_3355: A person who only reads books and has no worldly experience.
ext_3356: It refers to a young person who has only studied literature without any worldly experience.
ext_3357: Prioritizing public work over private matters.
ext_3358: It means prioritizing public work and putting private matters last, indicating an attitude that values public interest over personal gain.
ext_3359: One bears the consequences of their own actions.
ext_3360: Originating from the Buddhist concept that one receives the results of their own actions, it means that one fully bears the consequences of their actions.
ext_3361: Consequences and retribution follow according to the cause.
ext_3362: A core Buddhist concept that states causes and effects must correspond, meaning that good actions lead to good results, while bad actions lead to bad results.
ext_3364: It means like a candle in front of the wind, referring to a very dangerous and unstable position.
ext_3365: A situation that seems ready to explode with just a little provocation
ext_3366: It means that it will explode immediately with just a touch, indicating a very urgent situation where even a slight stimulus could lead to a major incident.
ext_3367: Rules or laws that must be followed
ext_3368: It means regulations made of gold and clauses made of jade, referring to rules or laws that are precious and must be strictly adhered to.
ext_3369: A season when the autumn sky is high and horses get fat
ext_3370: It means the sky is high and the horses are fat, expressing the rich and clear weather of the harvest season in autumn.
ext_3371: Great individuals take time to achieve
ext_3372: Originating from Laozi's teachings, it means that great vessels are completed slowly. It signifies that those who will achieve greatness succeed later.
ext_3373: Starting from low places to reach high places step by step
ext_3374: It means that to reach high places, one must start from low places, teaching that to accomplish great things, one must build a solid foundation step by step.
ext_3375: Difficulties piled on top of difficulties
ext_3376: It means frost added on snow, metaphorically indicating that overlapping difficulties lead to an even more challenging situation.
ext_3378: It means to bravely and vigorously move forward.
ext_3379: Showing off clumsy skills in front of experts
ext_3380: It means boasting an axe in front of the god of carpentry, Nopan, metaphorically referring to the foolishness of showing off skills in front of experts.
ext_3381: Repeating gathering and dispersing according to understanding.
ext_3382: It means to separate and gather, describing how people or groups come together and split apart.
ext_3383: The situation rapidly changes and unfolds uncontrollably.
ext_3384: It means that the situation suddenly takes a sharp downturn, indicating that events unexpectedly worsen or resolve quickly.
ext_3385: Going to great lengths to find excellent talent.
ext_3386: From the story of Liu Bei visiting Zhuge Liang's thatched cottage three times to recruit him, it signifies patiently and sincerely seeking talent.
ext_3387: Selecting the good and discarding the bad.
ext_3388: It means to take what is necessary and discard what is unnecessary, signifying the act of choosing the right option among many.
ext_3389: Bright wisdom that foresees the future.
ext_3390: It means having bright eyes that foresee ahead, referring to the exceptional insight to predict events before they happen.
ext_3391: Where there is merit, there is reward; where there is wrongdoing, there is punishment.
ext_3392: It means rewards are given with dignity and punishments are administered strictly, referring to the principle of fair rewards and punishments.
ext_3393: A tremendous difference as vast as heaven and earth.
ext_3394: It means a difference as vast as heaven and earth, metaphorically indicating a huge disparity between two subjects.
ext_3395: Internal chaos or conflict among allies.
ext_3396: It means chaos that arises within themselves, referring to conflicts among allies leading to internal strife.
ext_3397: Producing what is necessary by oneself.
ext_3398: It means to supply and fill oneself, indicating that one prepares what is needed without relying on others.
ext_3399: An old friend who played together riding bamboo horses in childhood.
ext_3400: It means an old friend who played together riding bamboo horses, referring to a very close friend who has grown up together since childhood.
ext_3401: Good medicine tastes bitter. Good advice may be unpleasant to hear but is helpful.
ext_3402: It means that good medicine tastes bitter, teaching that advice or truth may be unpleasant to hear but ultimately beneficial.
ext_3403: Changing one's perspective to think from the other person's point of view.
ext_3404: This means to think from the other person's perspective, embodying an empathetic attitude.
ext_3406: This means to act carelessly and indiscriminately, as if lifting something lightly and acting without thought.
ext_3407: Being in a bad environment leads to bad influences.
ext_3408: This warns that being close to bad people will lead to bad influences, as the saying goes, 'If you are near dirt, you will get dirty.'
ext_3410: This phrase originates from an anecdote where Xiang Yu asked Han Xin how many soldiers he could lead, and the answer was that the more, the better.
ext_3412: This means to go straight to the point without any preamble or introduction, as if charging in with just a sword.
ext_3413: Talented people will eventually be revealed.
ext_3414: This means that outstanding talent will eventually be revealed, even if hidden, originating from the story of Mozi, a retainer of Lord Pingyuan of Zhao, who recommended himself.
ext_3415: A world without worries, peaceful and tranquil.
ext_3416: This means that the world is peaceful, describing a state of tranquility without any worries.
ext_3417: Barely surviving after nine near-death experiences.
ext_3418: This means to narrowly escape from an extremely dangerous situation, having almost died nine times.
ext_3419: People in the same situation become allies.
ext_3420: This means that green and blue are the same color, indicating that people in similar situations or of the same kind come together.
ext_3421: A person who is versatile and impeccable in every aspect.
ext_3422: This means a person who is beautiful in all directions, excelling in various fields and adapting well to any situation.
ext_3423: The four sufferings of life: birth, aging, sickness, and death.
ext_3424: This refers to the four fundamental sufferings in Buddhism that all humans cannot escape: birth, aging, sickness, and death.
ext_3425: Continuously ongoing without day or night.
ext_3426: This means flowing like a long river day and night, indicating something that continues without rest.
ext_3427: Words and actions are consistent. Acting as one speaks.
ext_3428: This indicates a consistent attitude of practicing what one preaches, meaning words and actions align.
ext_3429: Loving the country and cherishing the people.
ext_3430: This means to love the country and the people, embodying a spirit of dedication to the nation and its citizens.
ext_3432: This indicates the ability to act freely without any hindrance or restriction.
ext_3433: There should be discernment between men and women.
ext_3434: This Confucian virtue emphasizes that there should be distinctions between men and women, highlighting the importance of etiquette and law.
ext_3435: All things are infinitely different from each other.
ext_3436: This means that there are a thousand differences and ten thousand distinctions, indicating that things are each unique and very diverse.
ext_3437: Learning the old to know the new.
ext_3438: This means that by mastering the old, one can understand the new, reflecting Confucius's teaching that one can become a teacher by learning the old.
ext_3440: This means extremely fast and instantaneous, likening it to the brief moment of lightning and the spark of flint.
ext_3441: Deceived by the difference right in front of you.
ext_3442: This refers to a story where a monkey got angry when offered 3 acorns in the morning and 4 in the evening, but was pleased when the offer was changed to 4 in the morning and 3 in the evening, indicating a fixation on superficial differences or being deceived by clever words.
ext_3443: In the past and now, both East and West
ext_3444: It refers to universal truths that apply anywhere, whether in the past or present, East or West.
ext_3446: It means learning and sometimes practicing, which is the first phrase of the Analects. It emphasizes the joy of consistently reviewing what has been learned.
ext_3447: People you meet will eventually part ways
ext_3448: A Buddhist truth that those who meet will inevitably part, meaning that all connections in the world are not eternal.
ext_3449: Feeling anxious or restless, unable to sit still in one place
ext_3450: It means that even when sitting, the place is uncomfortable, indicating that one cannot sit still due to worry or anxiety.
ext_3451: Strong roots lead to lush leaves. The foundation is important.
ext_3452: It means that strong roots lead to lush leaves, metaphorically indicating that a solid foundation results in abundant outcomes.
ext_3453: A strategy of forming alliances far away and attacking nearby.
ext_3454: A diplomatic strategy from the Warring States period, suggesting to befriend distant nations while attacking nearby ones.
ext_3455: Fight swiftly and reach a quick conclusion.
ext_3456: It means to fight quickly and decide quickly, indicating that matters are handled swiftly without prolonged delays.
ext_3457: Joining forces with a common purpose and forming brotherhood.
ext_3458: A famous anecdote from the Three Kingdoms where Liu Bei, Guan Yu, and Zhang Fei swore brotherhood in a peach blossom garden to achieve their shared goals.
ext_3459: Stretching one's neck like a crane and waiting earnestly
ext_3460: It means to stretch one's neck like a crane and wait painfully, indicating a deep yearning and anxious waiting.
ext_3461: Roads and transportation networks are unobstructed in all directions.
ext_3462: It means that roads and transportation are well connected in various directions.
ext_3463: Exploring the principles of things to broaden knowledge.
ext_3464: It means to deeply explore the principles of things to achieve understanding, which is the fundamental methodology of learning presented in the university.
ext_3465: Morally upright energy fills the heavens and the earth.
ext_3466: A concept mentioned by Mencius, meaning a vast and strong energy that fills the space between heaven and earth. It represents a broad and firm spirit based on morality.
ext_3468: It means that the thief wields a club, indicating the shameless attitude of someone who has committed a wrong but instead raises their voice or gets angry.
ext_3469: A virtuous mother and a good wife.
ext_3470: It expresses the ideal virtues in a family as a virtuous mother and a good wife.
ext_3471: Seeing the mountains while riding a horse. Glancing over without detail.
ext_3472: It means seeing the mountains while on a running horse, metaphorically indicating a cursory glance without careful observation.
ext_3473: No matter how cautious, a mistake can happen once.
ext_3474: It means that even after thinking a thousand times, a mistake can still occur, indicating that even the most careful person can make mistakes sometimes.
ext_3475: An unparalleled opportunity that comes once in a thousand years.
ext_3476: It means an opportunity that can be met once in a thousand years, indicating a rare and precious chance.
ext_3477: An act that is intolerable and enrages a thousand people.
ext_3478: It means that a thousand people are angry together, indicating that many people are outraged by an utterly unacceptable action.
ext_3479: Arrogant and looking down on others, unaware of the hierarchy.
ext_3480: It means that there are no people below one's eyes, indicating a shameless attitude that completely disregards others.
ext_3481: Busy running around in all directions.
ext_3482: It means running east and dashing west, indicating a flurry of activity in various directions.
ext_3484: This means that sweet things come after bitter ones, conveying a hopeful lesson that joy follows hardship.
ext_3485: If you prepare in advance, there is no worry
ext_3486: This means that if you are prepared in advance, there is no worry, emphasizing the importance of always being prepared.
ext_3487: Only the name exists, but there is no substance
ext_3488: It originated from the idea that only the name (reputation) is high, but there is no actual content.
ext_3489: Gaining two benefits from one thing
ext_3490: This is a phrase translated into Hanja after passing through Japan from the English proverb 'Killing two birds with one stone.'
ext_3491: Understanding without words, hearts and thoughts align
ext_3492: It originated from the anecdote where Buddha raised a lotus flower in silence during the sermon at Vulture Peak, and only his disciple Kashyapa understood the meaning and smiled.
ext_3493: Realizing truths that cannot be expressed in words
ext_3494: It refers to the same lotus anecdote of Buddha and Kashyapa, known as 'Ishimjunsim.'
ext_3495: Sharpening each other's knowledge and character
ext_3496: It means to continuously cultivate knowledge or character, likened to cutting jade, sweeping with a brush, chiseling with a tool, and grinding with a whetstone.
ext_3498: It means 'the spring breeze blowing in the ears,' metaphorically describing the attitude of ignoring others' words.
ext_3499: Progressing every day and developing every month
ext_3500: It means to progress (日) every day and develop (月) every month.
ext_3502: It originated from the teaching that the nature of the world is that when joyful events end, sorrowful events soon follow.
ext_3503: An object used as a bad example
ext_3504: This phrase was first used in a speech by Mao Zedong in the 1950s, meaning to reflect on negative examples and learn from them.
ext_3506: It means to successfully finish what you started and achieve good results.
ext_3507: A mulberry field turns into a blue sea. The world changes beyond recognition
ext_3508: This metaphor comes from the story where the fairy Mago said, 'I have seen the mulberry field turn into a blue sea three times,' indicating that the world changes beyond recognition.
ext_3509: Politics should be done with virtue. Governing the country with virtue
ext_3510: It originated from Confucius saying, 'Governing with virtue is like the North Star being in its place while all the other stars move toward it.'
ext_3511: Becoming an immortal and ascending to heaven. Freedom from the mundane world.
ext_3512: It means to grow wings and ascend to heaven, indicating a very good mood or suddenly leaving the mundane world.
ext_3513: Thinking ahead and preparing for danger while living comfortably
ext_3514: This teaches that the more comfortable you are, the more you should think about potential dangers and prepare in advance.
ext_3515: A close relationship that cannot be separated like fish and water.
ext_3516: This anecdote expresses deep friendship, where Liu Bei said to Zhuge Liang, 'Having you is like a fish having water.'
ext_3517: There must be loyalty between the king and his subjects
ext_3518: This is a core virtue of Confucianism, stating that there should be righteousness between the king and his subjects.
ext_3519: Eating the wind and sleeping in the dew. A difficult wandering life
ext_3520: It means to eat the wind and sleep in the dew, metaphorically describing a life of hardship while wandering for a greater purpose.
ext_3521: All human emotions such as joy, anger, sorrow, and pleasure.
ext_3522: This term encompasses the four basic human emotions: joy, anger, sorrow, and pleasure.
ext_3523: If the neighbor fails, my situation becomes precarious.
ext_3524: It originated from the story of the Qin state borrowing a road from the Wu state to attack the Guo state, where it was opposed with the saying, "If the lips are gone, the teeth will feel cold."
ext_3525: If you gain a profit, think first whether it is right.
ext_3526: This is Confucius's teaching to consider whether a profit aligns with righteousness first.
ext_3527: A harsh world where the strong dominate the weak.
ext_3528: It is a metaphor for the cruelty of the natural ecosystem where the strong consume the weak.
ext_3529: A term that encompasses both the East and the West, the past and the present.
ext_3530: It is a term that encompasses both the East and the West, the past and the present.
ext_3531: A public place where many people gather.
ext_3532: It means a large crowd gathered in a big and wide courtyard, referring to a public place with many people.
ext_3533: Emperors, feudal lords, generals, and prime ministers. Noble status.
ext_3534: It originated from the story of the rebellion against the class system during the rebellion of Jin Sheng and O Guang, where it was said, "Is there a different seed for kings and commoners?"
ext_3535: Helping each other and existing together in prosperity.
ext_3536: A modern slogan advocating for survival and prosperity together.
ext_3537: When getting married, the status, wealth, and environment of both families match.
ext_3538: It refers to the compatibility of the families, status, and economic power in traditional marriage customs.
ext_3539: A very close friendship that transcends interests.
ext_3540: It refers to an unchanging friendship that deeply understands each other beyond interests, as seen in the story of Guan Zhong and Po Su Ka during the Spring and Autumn period.
ext_3541: A very close relationship where life and death can be shared.
ext_3542: It refers to the friendship between Yin Xiangye and Yan Pa, who swore to share life and death by being able to behead each other.
ext_3543: A group gathered without discipline and order.
ext_3544: It refers to soldiers scattered disorderly like a flock of crows.
ext_3546: It means that hearts and intentions are perfectly in sync.
ext_3547: Diplomatic tactics that gather and disperse power based on interests.
ext_3548: It is a diplomatic strategy derived from the alliance strategy of the six states by Su Qin and the separate alliance strategy of Zhang Yi with the Qin state during the Warring States period.
ext_3549: Lessons or strict family education given by a father to a child.
ext_3550: It is the 'father's teaching' that originated from the story where Confucius sternly taught his son Bai Er to learn poetry and etiquette while passing through the courtyard.
ext_3551: Teaching that is kind and earnest to the point of being drilled into the ear.
ext_3552: It means to teach by pulling the ear and facing each other, indicating very kind and earnest teaching.
ext_3553: A beautiful scene where flower petals scatter chaotically.
ext_3554: It describes a beautiful landscape of peach blossoms falling like snow in an ideal place.
ext_3555: Lying down on a mat waiting for the king's decision or punishment.
ext_3556: It means lying down on a mat waiting for punishment, an action performed by officials in the Joseon period when asking the king for forgiveness for their sins.
ext_3557: Too many worries and concerns to sleep comfortably.
ext_3558: It means that the sleeping place is uncomfortable, indicating a state of anxiety or worry.
ext_3559: A cunning attitude to flatter others and win their favor.
ext_3560: It is Confucius's teaching that "among those who speak cunningly and decorate their expressions, virtuous people are rare."
ext_3561: Enticing others with sweet words and beneficial conditions.
ext_3562: It refers to sweet words and beneficial conditions crafted to please others.
ext_3563: So absurd that it cannot be expressed in words.
ext_3564: It means that the truth is so profound that it cannot be expressed in words, and later transformed to mean 'so absurd that one is at a loss for words.'
ext_3565: Recording important facts in large letters to stand out.
ext_3566: It means to treat a fact in large print especially prominently in newspapers or magazines.
ext_3567: Acting confidently according to one's beliefs without being swept away by the secular world.
ext_3568: It means to act according to one's firm beliefs without relying on or being swept away by others.
ext_3569: Be cautious of actions that may raise suspicion from the beginning.
ext_3570: A saying warning against actions that may lead to unjust suspicion, like not adjusting your shoes in a cucumber field or your hat under a plum tree.
ext_3572: Referring to the third son of the Jang family and the fourth son of the Lee family, it denotes ordinary and common people.
ext_3573: Deep in the night (between 11 PM and 1 AM).
ext_3574: It refers to the very deep night around midnight (between 11 PM and 1 AM).
ext_3575: Future generations that continue for a long time.
ext_3576: It means to continue for many generations into the distant future.
ext_3578: A term used to belittle a young and immature child, likening them to a baby bird with a yellow beak.
ext_3579: A very good day when luck and blessings come.
ext_3580: The most auspicious day in traditional fortune-telling when everything goes well and evil spirits flee.
ext_3581: A grand start but an unclear finish.
ext_3582: A saying from the Song Dynasty that compares a grand beginning like a dragon's head to a vague ending like a snake's tail.
ext_3583: Thorough and flawless from beginning to end.
ext_3584: It means to be thorough from head to tail, indicating no gaps from start to finish.
ext_3585: The other party's skills or knowledge develop to an astonishing degree.
ext_3586: An anecdote about the Wu general Lu Meng, who greatly advanced his studies, leading the scholar Lu Su to say, 'I rub my eyes and look again' in surprise.
ext_3587: A scene so terrible or embarrassing that one cannot bear to look.
ext_3588: A situation so horrific or pitiful that one cannot bear to see it with open eyes.
ext_3589: Devoting oneself solely to one task.
ext_3590: Focusing entirely on one thing and concentrating solely on one task.
ext_3591: A fleeting spring dream. Illusions or things that disappear without a trace.
ext_3592: A saying that likens the fleeting nature of life's wealth and glory to a fleeting spring dream.
ext_3593: When one dedicates their heart wholeheartedly, even heaven is moved.
ext_3594: It means that if one consistently puts their heart into their efforts, that sincere dedication will reach heaven and fulfill their wishes.
ext_3595: Writing or drawing without interruption in one go.
ext_3596: It refers to writing or drawing continuously without stopping the brush even once.
ext_3597: Catching everything all at once without missing anything.
ext_3598: It means to catch all the fish in one net, indicating capturing an entire group at once.
ext_3599: A favorable wind blows on the way, and things go smoothly.
ext_3600: A greeting wishing for a smooth and peaceful journey, like a boat sailing with a favorable wind.
ext_3601: Letting go seven times and catching again. Handling the other party freely.
ext_3602: An anecdote about Zhuge Liang capturing the king of Nanzhong, Meng Huo, seven times and letting him go seven times until he sincerely surrendered.
ext_3603: Fleeing is the best among the 36 stratagems.
ext_3604: A military treatise from China. It originates from the idea that among the thirty-six strategies, 'fleeing' is the best when in a disadvantageous situation.
ext_3605: Three heads and six arms. Superhuman abilities.
ext_3606: The appearance of deities like Asura in Buddhism. It signifies extraordinary abilities, having three heads and six arms.
ext_3607: A single hair among nine cows. A very small amount.
ext_3608: This phrase comes from a letter where Sima Qian lamented his insignificance by comparing his death to 'a single hair among nine cows' after enduring a humiliating punishment.
ext_3609: Everything will eventually return to the right result.
ext_3610: It is the principle that everything will ultimately return to the right path.
ext_3611: Completely unaware of the situation, like being in thick fog.
ext_3612: It means being unable to grasp the situation, originating from a story where a scholar named Janghae used magic to create fog for five miles and disappeared.
ext_3613: One can learn from others' mistakes.
ext_3614: It means that even rough stones from other mountains can be used to sharpen my jade, symbolizing that there are lessons to be learned from others' faults.
ext_3615: Acting as if no one is around.
ext_3616: This phrase originates from the assassin Xing Jia drinking and singing in the marketplace as if no one was around, meaning to act without restraint or rudeness.
ext_3618: A term referring to countless ordinary people.
ext_3619: Winning and losing in war is a common occurrence.
ext_3620: It means that winning and losing in battle is a frequent occurrence, encouraging not to be too discouraged by failure.
ext_3621: A winter fan and a summer stove. A metaphor for useless things.
ext_3622: A winter fan and a summer stove. It symbolizes things that are currently useless due to being out of season.
ext_3623: Grinding teeth and harboring resentment. Vowing revenge.
ext_3624: This phrase refers to the story of a person who was so angry about seeking revenge on Qin Shi Huang that they ground their teeth and harbored resentment.
ext_3625: Never before and never again. Unprecedented.
ext_3626: It means something is so rare and unique that it has never existed before and will not exist again.
ext_3627: The road ahead is ten thousand miles long. The future is infinitely bright.
ext_3628: It means that the path ahead is ten thousand miles long, signifying a bright and promising future.
ext_3629: Guzheng, poetry, and wine. The three companions of a scholar.
ext_3630: It means enjoying the company of the guzheng, wine, and poetry as three friends by the northern window.
ext_3631: No tree can withstand ten strikes.
ext_3632: This is a translation of the proverb 'No tree can withstand ten strikes,' meaning that consistent effort will lead to success.
ext_3634: It means having experienced all kinds of suffering and difficulties, signified by a thousand spicy flavors and ten thousand bitter tastes.
ext_3635: Empty theories that do not understand reality.
ext_3636: It refers to futile theories or discussions that do not align with reality.
ext_3637: Southern conquests and northern pacifications. Constant warfare.
ext_3638: It signifies roaming around to subdue external enemies.
ext_3639: Cutting off the head and tail. Speaking only the main point.
ext_3640: It means to cut off the head and tail, speaking only the main point without unnecessary explanations.
ext_3641: Placing the right person in the right position.
ext_3642: It means assigning a person with the right talent to the appropriate position.
ext_3643: I am in a hurry and have no time to care for others.
ext_3644: This is a translation of the proverb 'My nose is three feet long,' meaning I am in a hurry and cannot care for others.
ext_3645: Life is hanging by a thread. A very dangerous situation.
ext_3646: It is a very precarious situation where one's life is at stake in the blink of an eye.
ext_3647: Maintaining one's own opinions while living harmoniously.
ext_3648: It means to interact harmoniously with others without losing one's own perspective and blindly following.
ext_3649: The messenger has no news or response.
ext_3650: This originates from a historical event when envoys sent to bring back Yi Seong-gye, who had retired to Hamhung during the Joseon Dynasty, either did not return or were killed.
ext_3651: Torn apart and scattered in many directions.
ext_3652: Divided into four and torn into five. It depicts an organization or country that is fragmented and scattered.
ext_3653: No one to help, no matter where I look.
ext_3654: It means there are no relatives around, indicating a completely lonely situation with no one to rely on.
ext_3655: Something useless that is out of season.
ext_3656: A summer furnace and a winter fan. It metaphorically refers to things that are currently useless because they are out of season.
ext_3658: It refers to children who are filial to their parents and lovely descendants.
ext_3660: It depicts a peaceful era where one lives comfortably and enjoys their livelihood.
ext_3661: A sharp word that pierces the core of the opponent.
ext_3662: Like subduing a person with a weapon that is only an inch long, it metaphorically refers to piercing someone's heart or deeply moving them with a short and sharp word.
ext_3663: All kinds of rare foods from mountains and seas.
ext_3664: A very delicious dish made from all kinds of rare and precious ingredients from mountains and seas.
ext_3665: Hitting and colliding recklessly here and there. Charging in recklessly.
ext_3666: It means colliding to the left and right, indicating reckless behavior of hitting and crashing around.
ext_3667: Widely benefiting humanity. The founding ideology of Dangun Wanggeom.
ext_3668: 'To widely benefit the human world' is the founding ideology of Gojoseon.
ext_3669: Achieving what seems impossible through constant effort.
ext_3670: An old laborer quietly began to move the dirt from the mountain in front of his house, eventually moving the mountain and impressing the heavens, showing the power of steady effort.
ext_3671: Recovering strength after failure and challenging again.
ext_3672: Originating from a poem lamenting that Xiang Yu could rise again after defeat, it means to regain strength after failure and challenge again.
ext_3673: The flag is clear. The position or claim is clear.
ext_3674: It means the flag is clear, metaphorically indicating that one's attitude or position on a matter is very clear.
ext_3675: Repenting past mistakes and acting correctly now.
ext_3676: It means that what was wrong yesterday is right today, signifying repentance for past mistakes and returning to the right path.
ext_3677: Changing frequently morning and night. Lack of consistency.
ext_3678: It means changing what was decided in the morning again in the evening, indicating that plans or decisions change too frequently without consistency.
ext_3679: Sacrificing one's life to fulfill the way of benevolence.
ext_3680: This is the teaching of Confucius that 'one achieves benevolence by sacrificing oneself.'
ext_3681: Writing or books become very popular and rare.
ext_3682: This metaphor for a bestseller comes from a story where the writings of the leftist were so excellent that people competed to copy them, causing the price of paper in the capital, Luoyang, to rise.
ext_3683: An ideal place with beautiful scenery and a good living environment.
ext_3684: A blessed paradise with beautiful scenery where deities reside, connecting to the heavens.
ext_3686: Meaning a bridal chamber lit with splendid candles, referring to the first night of the newlyweds.
ext_3687: A third party benefits while two sides are in conflict.
ext_3688: An anecdote where a fisherman caught both a sandpiper and a clam while they were fighting, symbolizing a third party gaining benefits amidst a dispute.
ext_3689: Reading a sutra to a cow. No matter how much you say, it doesn't understand at all.
ext_3690: Reading a sutra to a cow. A very frustrating situation where no matter how much you teach, it cannot understand.
ext_3691: Rice cake in a picture. Looks good but is actually useless.
ext_3692: Rice cake in a picture. No matter how wonderful and appetizing it looks, it refers to something that cannot be possessed or is of no practical use.
ext_3693: Ruining a task by doing something unnecessary.
ext_3694: Derived from an anecdote where the first person to draw a snake in a game unnecessarily drew its legs and lost, referring to 'unnecessary legs.'
ext_3696: An anecdote where a shaman drew a dragon on a wall and added the eyes last, causing the dragon to come to life and fly away, meaning the most important final touch.
ext_3697: Not bending in the face of any difficulty.
ext_3698: Meaning that no matter how many times one is bent, they never yield, referring to not being discouraged by any difficulty.
ext_3699: A true friend who understands you deeply.
ext_3700: Meaning a friend who truly understands me, a close friend with whom I connect very well.
ext_3701: The wife who endured hardships together during poverty.
ext_3702: Derived from the story of Song Hong, who did not abandon his wife who endured poverty and hardship with him, eating leftover rice and chaff.
ext_3704: An anecdote where a woman was saved by her father's concubine, and later her deceased father helped her repay the kindness by tripping the enemy commander on the battlefield.
ext_3705: Hanging a sheep's head and selling dog meat. The outside and inside are different.
ext_3706: Meaning to hang a sheep's head on the door while actually selling dog meat, symbolizing deception where the appearance is impressive but the reality is poor.
ext_3707: Inconsistency between one's words and actions.
ext_3708: Meaning to contradict oneself, where the words or actions of the same person do not match and are contradictory.
ext_3709: One's words and actions bind oneself.
ext_3710: Meaning to bind oneself with the rope one has spun, referring to getting into a difficult situation because of one's own words or actions.
ext_3711: The achievement of studying steadily in a difficult environment.
ext_3712: An anecdote of Cha Yoon and Son Gang, who succeeded after enduring hardships reading by the light of fireflies and their own eyes due to poverty.
ext_3713: Blood and energy are at their peak.
ext_3714: Confucius' teaching that in youth, one is full of vigor and likes to quarrel, so one must be cautious of this.
ext_3716: Referring to conflicts among family members who share blood, such as parents or siblings.
ext_3717: The outside and inside are different. Words and actions do not match.
ext_3718: Meaning that the outside and inside are different, where actions or feelings are inconsistent.
ext_3719: Sharp meanings hidden in ordinary words.
ext_3720: Meaning that there is substance in ordinary words, referring to deep meanings or sharp criticisms hidden in seemingly simple words.
ext_3721: Words do not make sense. Completely illogical statements.
ext_3722: Words that do not hold together in one logic or reasoning. Absurd statements that do not match.
ext_3724: A deep state of immersion where one focuses solely on reading books and has no other thoughts.
ext_3725: Reading books and befriending ancient sages.
ext_3726: It means gaining knowledge by making great people from the past your friends through reading.
ext_3728: Hiding pain or anger without showing it outwardly, being cautious and enduring.
ext_3729: Similar levels that are hard to distinguish.
ext_3730: Derived from the story where Taegu, the grandfather, said, 'It's hard to say the elder is better or the younger is better' when the two sons of Jinsik quarreled, meaning it's difficult to determine superiority.
ext_3731: Sprouting abundantly like bamboo shoots after rain.
ext_3732: It metaphorically describes a situation where many things happen at once, like bamboo shoots sprouting after rain.
ext_3733: Pretending to follow outwardly while betraying inwardly.
ext_3734: Having a mind to betray while pretending to obey in front of someone.
ext_3735: Completely out of touch with no contact.
ext_3736: Completely cut off from news or letters, with no way to know.
ext_3737: Regretting that parents have passed away when one wants to show filial piety.
ext_3738: It means that while one wishes to be filial, they lament that their parents have passed away, like a tree wanting to be still but the wind does not cease.
ext_3740: It means that when a fox dies, it lays its head towards the hill it lived on, symbolizing the instinct of animals and the hearts of humans longing for their hometown.
ext_3741: Unlucky people encounter obstacles even with good opportunities.
ext_3742: An anecdote about the poor Prime Minister Hwanghee receiving eggs from the king, but the boiled eggs were rotten and inedible, symbolizing that unlucky people cannot seize good opportunities.
ext_3743: Feeling pity for those suffering from the same illness.
ext_3744: It means that people suffering from the same illness feel deep sympathy for each other.
ext_3745: Living long and healthy without illness.
ext_3746: It means living very long and healthy without any illness.
ext_3747: Using only part of a text and misusing its original meaning.
ext_3748: Ignoring the context of someone else's writing or speech and forcibly quoting only a part that suits one's taste.
ext_3749: Not knowing what is close by.
ext_3750: The proverb 'It's dark under the lamp.' It symbolizes not knowing well about things nearby.
ext_3751: Not knowing even a single character.
ext_3752: A very ignorant person who does not know even a single character.
ext_3753: Greatly surprised and losing color in the face.
ext_3754: Becoming pale in the face due to being greatly surprised.
ext_3756: An object or person that has no use or value at all.
ext_3759: Similar in a broad sense but different in small details. Almost the same.
ext_3760: It means that in a broad view, they are the same, but only very small parts are different, indicating they are almost similar.
ext_3761: A single word and half a phrase. Very short speech.
ext_3762: It means a single word or half a phrase, used to strongly indicate that even a very short word or response is absent.
ext_3763: There is neither better nor worse.
ext_3764: The difference in skill is so small that it is hard to tell which is above and which is below.
ext_3766: Exaggerating something as big as a club when it is only the size of a needle.
ext_3767: The opinions of many people agree in one voice.
ext_3768: It means that although the mouths are different, the voices are the same, indicating that many people's opinions or words are in perfect agreement.
ext_3769: Orderly without a single strand out of place.
ext_3770: It means that not even a single thread is tangled, indicating that order is maintained without any disorder.
ext_3771: Only harmful and not beneficial at all.
ext_3772: Only harmful and not beneficial at all.
ext_3773: Despairing and giving up on oneself without looking back.
ext_3774: Despairing and neglecting oneself, giving up everything.
ext_3775: Very diverse in appearance or form.
ext_3776: It means that the shapes or phenomena of worldly things are extremely diverse, in thousands or tens of thousands of ways.
ext_3777: A lightning strike from a clear sky. A sudden major disaster.
ext_3778: A lightning strike from a clear sky. A sudden major disaster or shocking event.
ext_3779: Farming during the day and studying at night.
ext_3780: Farming during the day and reading books at night, studying hard even in a busy environment.
ext_3781: The ups and downs of life are hard to predict.
ext_3782: An anecdote about an old man from the border who brought back a fine horse after it ran away, and although his son was injured because of that horse, he avoided conscription, meaning that the ups and downs of life are unpredictable.
ext_3784: Seeing things in reality creates a desire to possess them.
ext_3785: The person who caused the trouble must resolve it.
ext_3786: It means that the one who tied the knot must untie it, indicating that the person responsible for the trouble must resolve it directly.
ext_3787: When commanded from above, those below obey. A strict hierarchy.
ext_3788: The superior commands and the subordinate obeys.
ext_3789: Adding fuel to the fire. Making a bad situation worse.
ext_3790: Adding fuel to the fire. It is a metaphor for exacerbating disaster or anger.
ext_3791: Sneaking away in the middle of the night.
ext_3792: Sneaking away in the middle of the night.
ext_3793: Only those who adapt well to their environment survive.
ext_3794: It is the natural law that only those who adapt to their environment survive.
ext_3795: One by one, in detail. The story is long and tight.
ext_3796: Every detail in speech or writing is very detailed.
ext_3797: Each person has different characteristics, thoughts, and personalities.
ext_3798: Each person has different personalities or thoughts.
ext_3799: The care and preparation are thorough without any gaps.
ext_3800: The care is thorough, leaving no gaps at all.
ext_3801: Not considering it important and neglecting it.
ext_3802: Neglecting something or an event without considering it important.
ext_3803: Sincerely obeying in your heart and acknowledging with your mouth.
ext_3804: It means to be deeply convinced in your heart and to respond with your mouth, truly agreeing and supporting.
ext_3806: It refers to following old and bad habits (evil customs) that have been passed down since ancient times without change.
ext_3807: Four sages who secluded themselves away from the secular world. Noble hermits.
ext_3808: It refers to the four wise men (elders) who lived in hiding on Mount Shangshan to escape the chaotic times at the end of the Qin dynasty.
ext_3809: The books of the Three Sovereigns and Five Emperors. Refers to the oldest classics.
ext_3810: It refers to the very excellent and ancient legendary classics from the era of the Three Sovereigns and Five Emperors in ancient China.
ext_3811: Low in status but excellent in argument and reasonable.
ext_3812: It means that although one's social status is low, their words and opinions are excellent and reasonable.
